name: org.machanism.machai:genai-client
description:
GenAI Client (org.machanism.machai:genai-client) is a provider-agnostic Java library (MachAI module) that standardizes integration with Generative AI / LLM backends while keeping application code isolated from vendor SDKs. It exposes a small core SPI, org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Genai, for conversation-style prompting and execution: initialize with org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator (init), append user prompts (prompt) and system instructions (instructions), attach local java.io.File or remote java.net.URL resources (addFile overloads), register host-executed tools/functions for model function calling (addTool), execute (perform) and read per-run token usage metrics (usage via org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Usage). Providers may keep session state and can be reset via clear(). Providers can optionally log input payloads using inputsLog(java.io.File) and pass a host working directory to tool handlers via setWorkingDir(java.io.File).

Provider resolution and lifecycle: org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenaiProviderManager.getProvider(java.lang.String chatModel, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator conf) resolves providers from an identifier formatted as "Provider:Model" (e.g., "OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini"). If the provider prefix is omitted (e.g., "gpt-4o-mini"), it defaults to the offline None provider (org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.none.NoneProvider). Provider class resolution supports short names mapped to org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.<providerLower>.<Provider>Provider or fully-qualified provider class names. The manager sets conf key "chatModel" to the model portion and calls provider.init(conf). Usage aggregation: providers can add usage records to GenaiProviderManager.addUsage(Usage), and GenaiProviderManager.logUsage() logs an aggregate summary (note: aggregation is stored in a static java.util.ArrayList and is not synchronized).

Built-in providers:
- org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.openai.OpenAIProvider: functional provider using the OpenAI Java SDK (Responses API). Supports prompts, system instructions, local file uploads (Files API, purpose USER_DATA) and URL-file references, embedding generation (embedding(text, dimensions)), tool/function calling (register tools with addTool and dispatch function calls to host ToolFunction handlers), and iterative tool-call resolution (follow-up requests until final text). It captures ResponseUsage (input tokens, cached input tokens, output tokens) and automatically aggregates it via GenaiProviderManager.addUsage(). OpenAIProvider configuration keys include: chatModel (required), OPENAI_API_KEY (required), OPENAI_BASE_URL (optional OpenAI-compatible endpoint), GENAI_TIMEOUT (seconds; optional default 600), MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS (optional default 300000), MAX_TOOL_CALLS (optional default 200). Tool handlers in OpenAIProvider are invoked with an Object[] of length 2: (0) com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JsonNode arguments and (1) java.io.File working directory.
- org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.codemie.CodeMieProvider: EPAM CodeMie integration that performs an OIDC token request to obtain an OAuth2 access token (password grant if GENAI_USERNAME contains "@", otherwise client_credentials). It sets OPENAI_BASE_URL to the CodeMie API base URL and OPENAI_API_KEY to the access token, then delegates by chatModel prefix: gpt-* (or blank) -> OpenAIProvider; gemini-* -> GeminiProvider; claude-* -> ClaudeProvider. Optional AUTH_URL overrides the token endpoint. It rejects configurations where OPENAI_API_KEY is set via environment variable to prevent conflicts.
- org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.none.NoneProvider: offline/no-op provider used as default when provider prefix is omitted. It accumulates prompt text and can write prompts to an inputsLog file and write instructions to instructions.txt alongside the log. perform() always returns null; embedding throws UnsupportedOperationException; tools and files are ignored; usage is always zero.
- org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.gemini.GeminiProvider and org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.claude.ClaudeProvider: placeholders/scaffolds; most operations are not implemented yet.

Host-integrated tools (function calling): the library provides an SPI org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools to install tool sets into a provider. Tool sets are discovered via java.util.ServiceLoader and applied using org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionToolsLoader. Tool sets can optionally receive a Configurator for runtime placeholder substitution in strings using ${...}.
Included tool sets:
- org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FileFunctionTools: registers read_file_from_file_system, write_file_to_file_system, list_files_in_directory, get_recursive_file_list. Paths are interpreted relative to the working directory supplied by the provider/tool invocation.
- org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.WebFunctionTools: registers get_web_content (HTTP GET with optional headers, timeout, charset, selector extraction, and optional textOnly rendering) and call_rest_api (generic HTTP method with headers/body). Supports basic auth via URL userInfo and ${...} placeholder substitution in URLs/headers.
- org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.CommandFunctionTools: registers run_command_line_tool and terminate_process. Command execution uses ProcessBuilder with working-directory confinement under the project root, bounded output capture (LimitedStringBuilder), optional env parsing, and an OS-specific deny-list heuristic via CommandSecurityChecker (denylist/unix.txt or denylist/windows.txt, extendable via configuration property ft.command.denylist). terminate_process throws a ProcessTerminationException with an exitCode.

Adapter/decorator support: org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenaiAdapter is a delegating Genai base class that forwards all operations to a configured delegate provider, enabling cross-cutting concerns (logging, metrics, retries) while preserving the Genai contract.

Typical usage: resolve a provider via org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenaiProviderManager.getProvider("OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini", conf); optionally install tools via org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionToolsLoader.getInstance().setConfiguration(conf) and applyTools(provider); setWorkingDir(new java.io.File(".")) if tools are used; set instructions and prompts; attach files if needed; call perform(); then read provider.usage() and/or aggregate with GenaiProviderManager.logUsage().

constructors.description: Resolves and instantiates a Genai provider based on a provider/model identifier. The identifier is typically formatted as "Provider:Model" (for example, "OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini"). If the provider part is omitted, the default provider (NoneProvider) is used. Providers are created via reflection (public no-arg constructor), the Configurator is updated with chatModel (model-only part), and provider.init(conf) is called.

examples.description: Creates and uses an OpenAI-backed provider instance, runs a prompt, and reads back token usage.

constructors.description: Returns a singleton loader that discovers FunctionTools implementations via java.util.ServiceLoader and can apply them to a Genai provider. The loader can also distribute a Configurator to tools for runtime placeholder substitution (for example, ${...} in web headers or command strings).

examples.description: Installs all host-integrated tools (file, web, command) into a provider instance.

constructors.signature: java.lang.String org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.codemie.CodeMieProvider.getToken(java.lang.String url, java.lang.String username, java.lang.String password) throws java.io.IOException
constructors.description: Requests an OAuth 2.0 access token from the configured CodeMie OpenID Connect token endpoint. Uses password grant when username contains "@" and client_credentials otherwise. Intended for configuring OPENAI_API_KEY for downstream OpenAI-compatible calls.

examples.description: Obtains an access token directly from the token endpoint (typically used internally by CodeMieProvider).

features.name: Genai.perform
features.description: Executes the provider using accumulated prompts, instructions, attached files, and registered tools to produce a response. Concrete providers may perform multiple iterations when tool calls are involved (for example, OpenAIProvider executes tool calls, appends tool outputs, and re-queries until a final response is produced).

examples.description: Runs a model invocation using prompts and system instructions.

features.name: Genai.addTool
features.signature: void org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Genai.addTool(java.lang.String name, java.lang.String description, org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Genai.ToolFunction function, java.lang.String... paramsDesc)
features.description: Registers a host-executed tool/function that a provider may expose to the model for function calling. ToolFunction receives an Object[] and returns an Object result. In OpenAIProvider, the tool handler receives (0) a com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JsonNode of parsed arguments and (1) a java.io.File working directory.

examples.description: Registers a simple tool that reads a JSON argument and returns a string result.

features.name: Genai.embedding
features.description: Computes an embedding vector for the input text using a provider-specific embedding model and dimensionality. Implemented by OpenAIProvider; unsupported by NoneProvider (throws UnsupportedOperationException).

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Genai provider = /* OpenAIProvider */;
java.util.List<java.lang.Double> vec = provider.embedding("hello world", 1536L);
examples.description: Computes an embedding vector for downstream semantic search or similarity matching.

features.name: GenaiProviderManager.logUsage
features.description: Aggregates token usage records across runs and logs a summary of input, cached input, and output tokens. Usage aggregation is stored in a static list and is not synchronized.

examples.description: Logs total token usage across multiple provider invocations.

features.name: FunctionToolsLoader.applyTools
features.description: Discovers FunctionTools implementations via ServiceLoader and registers all tool functions into a provider. Tool sets typically include file tools, web tools, and command execution/termination tools.

examples.description: Discovers and applies all tool installers to a provider.

features.name: WebFunctionTools.getWebContent
features.description: Host-side tool implementation for get_web_content: performs an HTTP GET to a URL, applies optional headers, timeout, charset decoding, optional CSS selector extraction, and optional HTML-to-text rendering. Supports basic authentication via URL userInfo converted to an Authorization header and supports ${...} placeholder substitution in URLs/headers when a Configurator is supplied.

examples.description: Registers web tools so the provider can expose them to function-calling capable models.

customizations.name: Genai provider implementations
customizations.description: Add a new provider by implementing org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Genai with a public no-arg constructor. It can then be resolved by GenaiProviderManager either via a short name (org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.<lowercase>.<Name>Provider) or a fully-qualified class name in the provider portion of the model identifier.

examples.description: Skeleton of a new provider implementation.

customizations.name: FunctionTools SPI
customizations.description: Extend the host-executed tool surface by implementing org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools and registering it via ServiceLoader (META-INF/services/org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools). Tool installers register one or more tools into a Genai provider via Genai.addTool. Installers may also accept a Configurator to resolve ${...} placeholders at runtime.

examples.description: Adds a new host-side function tool installer.

customizations.name: Command deny-list extension
customizations.description: Extend the built-in OS-specific command deny-list used by CommandFunctionTools/CommandSecurityChecker via configuration property ft.command.denylist. The property is applied as a String.format template where the first argument is the built-in denylist content, enabling prepend/append of custom rules.

examples.description: Appends extra keyword rules to the built-in deny-list.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Genai
studs.description: Provider contract for integrating a concrete GenAI backend. Implementations collect prompts/instructions, attachments, and tools, execute perform() to produce a response, and report per-run token usage. The interface also defines a ToolFunction functional interface for host-executed tools, plus common constants such as GENAI_USERNAME / GENAI_PASSWORD and prompt separators.

examples.description: Uses the Genai contract without binding to a specific vendor implementation (defaults to NoneProvider when no provider prefix is specified).

methods: void addTool(java.lang.String name, java.lang.String description, org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Genai.ToolFunction function, java.lang.String... paramsDesc)

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools
studs.description: SPI for installing host-side function tools into a Genai provider, typically discovered via ServiceLoader and applied through FunctionToolsLoader. Includes helper support for resolving ${...} placeholders using a Configurator via replace(String, Configurator).

examples.description: Discovers and applies all tool installers to a provider.

examples.title: Resolve provider and run a prompt
Enable host tools for function calling
Use CodeMieProvider for enterprise authentication
